name: ExtensionAppointment_Priority
title: R5: priority additional types
description: R5: `Appointment.priority` additional types (CodeableConcept) additional types from child elements (coding, text)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `Appointment.priority` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`Appointment.priority` 0..1 `CodeableConcept`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `Appointment.priority` 0..1 `CodeableConcept`
*  R4B: `Appointment.priority` 0..1 `unsignedInt`
*  R4: `Appointment.priority` 0..1 `unsignedInt`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `Appointment.priority` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Appointment.priority` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The mappings for `Appointment.priority` do not cover the following types: CodeableConcept.
The mappings for `Appointment.priority` do not cover the following types based on type expansion: coding, text.

element.definition: R5: `Appointment.priority` additional types (CodeableConcept) additional types from child elements (coding, text)
element.comment:

The mappings for `Appointment.priority` do not cover the following types: CodeableConcept.

Seeking implementer feedback on this property and how interoperable it is.Using an extension to record a CodeableConcept for named values may be tested at a future connectathon.

element.short: Used to make informed decisions if needing to re-prioritize
element.definition: The priority of the appointment. Can be used to make informed decisions if needing to re-prioritize appointments. (The iCal Standard specifies 0 as undefined, 1 as highest, 9 as lowest priority).
element.comment: Seeking implementer feedback on this property and how interoperable it is.Using an extension to record a CodeableConcept for named values may be tested at a future connectathon.

binding.description: Indicates the urgency of the appointment.
